Empires & Puzzles Onyx Review: Is The Ninja of Elusive Winds Still Viable?

Remember when Onyx was the scariest thing in the shadows? We revisit the Ninja of Elusive Winds to see if his legendary Buff Block and Dispel can still cut it in a world of 4000 HP monsters. Spoiler: He's squishy, but he's got tricks.
Onyx: The Purple Ninja Who Hates Your Buffs
Onyx: The Ninja Turtle's Arch-Nemesis Who forgot to Go to the Gym
Onyx burst onto the scene in late 2020 as part of the original Ninja Tower wave, introducing the chaotic "Charge" mana speed to
Ranger, he was designed to be a flexible Swiss Army knife of destruction—sniping at 1x charge or wiping teams at 3x.
Back in the day, he was a monster. Today? He's a tactical specialist. While his damage has fallen behind the curve, his utility—specifically the rare combination of
—keeps him relevant for players who like to control the board rather than just brute force it.

Onyx rocks the classic "suburban ninja" aesthetic. Clad in purple tactical gear with a cowl that screams "I brood on gargoyles," his defining feature is the set of Wolverine-style claws on his gauntlets. It’s a sharp, aggressive design that fits his fast, slashing playstyle. Compared to the more mystical or over-the-top designs of recent heroes (looking at you, Construct family), Onyx feels grounded—like he’s actually here to do a job, not just float around glowing.
Let's not sugarcoat it: Onyx's stats are a relic of a bygone era.

The "Charge" mechanic is Onyx's saving grace. It gives you options:
A surgical strike. Hits one target, dispels them, and blocks buffs. This is incredible for shutting down a taunting tank or a healer about to fire.
Hits three. Ideally used to control a flank and tank simultaneously.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Ninja family was the first to introduce the "Dodge" meta, and it remains annoying to this day.
(Special Skills AND Normal Attacks) is a lifesaver for a hero with such low defense.
The chance to counter is a nice bonus, but don't rely on it for damage.
Onyx comes from the era before "Passives" were stapled onto every hero. He has
innate passives outside of his Family Bonus.

At Level 90, he gets a raw attack boost for the first 6 turns. This helps patch his outdated damage stats, allowing him to hit slightly harder in the opening rush. It’s decent, but "Dodge" or "Damage Reduction" might have been better to keep him alive.
In 2020/2021, Onyx was a god. The flexibility to snipe or wipe a team made him a top-tier defender and attacker. Players feared the "Ninja Tower" defense teams.
The stat gap is undeniable. He gets one-shot by modern snipers. His damage tickles modern tanks.
He is no longer viable on defense (too squishy). However, on offense, he is a
hero. You bring him to shut down Ludwigs, Aramis, or any heavy buffer. He is a tool, not a weapon.
You have a tough choice: Polish the glass cannon or try to make the glass bulletproof?
Maximizes his damage potential. Since his multipliers (270%-380%) are decent, every point of Attack helps him actually kill things.
He remains extremely fragile. A stiff breeze will kill him.
Keeps him alive long enough to fire his x2 or x3 charge. Enhances the value of his Dodge family bonus.
His damage becomes negligible. He becomes purely a utility dispeller.

damage while he blocks buffs. Plus, his Ranger class "Pierce" talent scales with damage.
Can fire early to save a match or hold for a wipe.
One of the best ailments in the game. Stops HoT, Taunt, Dodge, Retaliate, etc.
The "Pierce" talent (25% chance to bypass defensive buffs) allows him to hit through Defense Up or Elemental barriers, ensuring his damage lands before the dispel triggers.

On defense teams, the AI fires randomly (sometimes at x1, sometimes holds for x3), making him unreliable.
Can no longer be relied upon as a primary damage dealer.
Excellent for dissecting specific defenses that rely on buffs (e.g., teams with heavy healers or taunters).
He is a hard counter to the entire format. He prevents the enemy from getting buffs, neutralizing the tournament rule.
Blocking defensive buffs prevents the enemy from mitigating damage since they can't heal.
Onyx works best when paired with heroes who can keep him alive or capitalize on his soft-up.
Activates the Family Bonus for maximum Dodge chance. Garnet provides the Overheal needed to keep Onyx's low HP pool irrelevant.
Mana boosters help Onyx reach his x2 or x3 charge much faster, bypassing the slow tile gain. Xnolphod (even post-nerf) works well with Purple stacks.
Proteus stops mana, Onyx stops buffs. The enemy literally cannot do anything.
(e.g., Gegneeshu, Toon Joon) will delete Onyx before he charges.
(e.g., Xiahou Dun) prevent him from ever reaching x3 charge.
Don't pair him with other "Overwrite" ailments if you need the Buff Block. For example, don't overwrite his Buff Block with a less useful ailment if the enemy is about to buff themselves.

If you pull Onyx early, rejoice! He is fantastic for map stages and mid-tier raids. His flexibility teaches you how to manage the board. Prioritize his attack stats so he can carry you through story mode bosses by blocking their devastating buffs.

He is a "Luxury Tool." Do not Limit Break 2 (Alpha Aether) him unless you have a surplus of mats and a deep love for Ninjas. He does his job (Dispel/Block) just fine at 4/80 or 4/85. The stat boost from LB2 won't make him a meta defender again. Use him in your 4th or 5th War team to counter specific Ludwig tanks.
The community has had a rollercoaster relationship with Onyx.
"He is a monster. 803 Attack is ridiculous!" —
"Onyx + Cobalt is the end of Empires & Puzzles." —
"His stats are pure garbage even with +25 emblems. He's a bench warmer." —
One player noted a key mechanic:
"I didn't know that bypass gets around taunt... that makes him really good against Black Knight tanks."
This remains true today—his Ranger talent is his secret weapon against the Taunt meta.

Onyx is like an old sports car. He doesn't have the safety features or the horsepower of the new models, and if you crash him, he crumples. But man, is he still fun to drive when the conditions are right. He brings a level of control and finesse that few "smash and grab" modern heroes can match.
Keep him, emblem him, but maybe don't bet the house on him. He's the perfect solution to a problem you'll face about once every two wars.
A 2026 deep dive into Onyx, the original Purple Ninja. We analyze his Charge skills, buff blocking utility, and whether his stats can survive the modern meta.
